title: phebalium glandulosum subsp. macrocalyx
intro: Phebalium glandulosum subsp. macrocalyx is a rare and endangered plant species, belonging to the Rutaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Australian bush medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Phebalium glandulosum subsp. macrocalyx include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water as a tea, and in some cases, it is also used in topical applications for its antiseptic properties.
